summary: Tested Apotheosis ACE banquet app (Django + MongoDB + React). 21/22 backend tests pass. Critical backend bug found in order update (2-min lock window). Welcome, Gallery, Guest registration pages render correctly with proper French verses/gold-green theme. Admin login UI did not appear to redirect to dashboard after submit in browser test.

critical.issue: Returns 500: "can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes". `locked_at` is stored as timezone-aware UTC datetime but MongoDB returns naive datetime on read; comparison with `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` raises TypeError. This breaks the entire 2-minute modification feature - guests cannot modify their dish selection.

minor.issue: Unauthenticated calls return 500 (with exception detail leaked) instead of 401/403. The `get_current_user` exception is caught in a broad `except Exception as e: return 500`. Should return 401 explicitly and avoid leaking internal exception messages.
Same pattern - returns 401 only by coincidence because the outer except handles it. Refactor to use proper HTTP status codes consistently across admin-protected endpoints.

ui_bugs.issue: After filling admin@apotheosis.com / apotheosis2026 and clicking 'Se connecter', the URL remained at /admin/login and dashboard content was not detected. Backend login API works (verified via pytest). Possible issue: client may not be sending credentials with fetch (missing credentials: 'include') or not handling cookie-based session correctly, or redirect logic missing.

integration_issues.issue: Login appears not to navigate to dashboard. Backend sets httpOnly cookie successfully. Verify frontend axios/fetch uses withCredentials:true and React Router navigates after success.

issues: Gold/green/black/white theme correctly applied with Cormorant Garamond + Outfit fonts. French verses present on every page. Château background image visible. Design looks good.

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: In /app/backend/api/views.py update_order_view (line 259) - normalize datetimes before compare. Either store locked_at as naive UTC, or replace tzinfo on read: `locked_at = order['locked_at'].replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if order['locked_at'].tzinfo is None else order['locked_at']`

FIX HIGH: Verify AdminLoginPage actually navigates after successful login. Ensure axios/fetch uses credentials:'include' or withCredentials:true so httpOnly cookie is set on the browser. Add navigate('/admin/dashboard') after successful POST /api/auth/login.
Improve auth error handling: return 401 explicitly when token missing/invalid (currently returns 500 with leaked exception messages on protected endpoints).
critical_code_review_comments: views.py uses broad try/except returning 500 on auth failures - leaks internal exception text and obscures real error codes
Datetime tz handling is inconsistent: writes are tz-aware, reads from MongoDB are tz-naive. Centralize a helper to normalize tz on read.
register_guest_view allows unlimited duplicate guests with same name+table (no uniqueness check); could be intentional but worth noting
create_order_view returns datetime objects which Django serializes OK, but ensure frontend parses ISO strings correctly
No CSRF protection on cookie-auth endpoints (all are @csrf_exempt). For production add CSRF-token header check on state-changing requests.

success_rate.frontend: 75% (3/4 main pages verified; admin login redirect failed)

seed_data_creation: Created test tables 9001/9002/9003, test dish 'TEST_Poulet Rôti', test guest 'TEST_Jean' with order on table 9003. Data persists in MongoDB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes (1) tz comparison in update_order_view and (2) admin login redirect, retest: PUT /api/orders/<id>/update should return 200, and frontend /admin/login should navigate to dashboard. Use existing test file /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Test data with TEST_ prefix is in DB.
rca of the issue: Order update 500 RCA: MongoDB's pymongo by default returns datetime objects without tzinfo (naive) even when written with timezone.utc. Line 259 `if now > order['locked_at']` mixes aware (now) with naive (locked_at) -> TypeError. Fix: use MongoClient(tz_aware=True) in mongo_client.py, OR normalize on read: `locked_at = order['locked_at'].replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)`. Reproduction: register guest -> create order -> immediately PUT /api/orders/<id>/update -> observe 500.
